task_completion.description: Verify that the LibraryApp project and the target Java file are opened in the IDE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The IDE (e.g., Eclipse) is open, showing the 'LibraryApp' project in the project explorer, and 'LibraryApp.java' is open in the main editor.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Externalize Strings' wizard or dialog is launched for the target file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Externalize Strings' is visible on the screen, displaying a list of strings extracted from LibraryApp.java.

task_completion.description: Verify that the resource bundle and accessor class are correctly named and located.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Externalize Strings wizard or the project explorer, the properties file is named 'messages.properties' and the accessor class is named 'Messages.java', both located under the 'com.library.app' package.

task_completion.description: Verify that technical strings (like switch case constants) are marked to not be externalized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Externalize Strings wizard, numeric strings like '1', '2', etc., are marked with a 'Never Translate' / 'Ignore' status (often indicated by a crossed-out icon or specific text in the status column).

task_completion.description: Verify that user-facing strings are marked for externalization and populated in the properties file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Externalize Strings wizard, user-facing strings (prompts, menu options) are marked as 'Translate'. Alternatively, the 'messages.properties' file is shown containing multiple key-value pairs (at least 15 entries).

task_completion.description: Verify that all modified files are saved and the project compiles without errors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Editor tabs for LibraryApp.java, Messages.java, and messages.properties do not have unsaved indicators (like an asterisk '*'). There are no red error squiggles in the code editor and no red error markers in the Problems view or Project Explorer.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the IDE's GUI tools to externalize strings rather than terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the IDE's Externalize Strings dialog and refactoring tools, rather than terminal windows executing bash or python scripts to manipulate the files.

integrity.description: Verify that the accessor class and properties file appear to be generated by the IDE.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Messages.java file contains standard IDE-generated boilerplate code (e.g., extending NLS and calling NLS.initializeMessages in Eclipse) rather than being manually typed or copy-pasted.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with a full Java IDE.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of a full IDE interface (like Eclipse) with a Project Explorer, Outline, and Problems views, rather than a basic text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'src/main/java/com/library/app/LibraryApp.java' inside the '/home/ga/eclipse-workspace/LibraryApp' project. The expected properties file is 'src/main/java/com/library/app/messages.properties' and the expected accessor class is 'src/main/java/com/library/app/Messages.java'. There are 17 user-facing strings in the file. The properties file must contain at least 15 entries for full credit. Technical strings (like switch cases) should not be externalized.
